summary: Epic Dark Star sandwich, soaring jams and tight rhythm section
blurb: The show delivers a monumental Dark Star > Attics Of My Life > Dark Star > Sugar Magnolia > Dark Star sequence, backed by a thunderous Not Fade Away into Easy Wind and a seamless Saint Stephen > China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider medley. Jerry’s lead is on fire throughout, while Phil and the dual drums lock in solidly. The encore closes with a heartfelt Swing Low (Sweet Chariot).
review: June 24, 1970 at the Capitol Theater captured one of the Dead's most electrifying late‑summer outings. From the opening burst of Not Fade Away into an inspired Easy Wind, the band launched into a three‑part Dark Star sandwich that rides through Attics Of My Life and erupts into a jubilant Sugar Magnolia before spiraling back into another Dark Star climax – a performance many fans describe as “transcendent” and “smoking”. The second set kept the momentum rolling with a flawless Saint Stephen > China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider flow, showcasing tight interplay between Jerry’s soaring leads and Bob’s rhythm work. Phil’s bass added depth and harmonic richness throughout, while Mickey and Bill functioned as a relentless percussion machine that drove every jam forward. The evening wound down with an intimate Swing Low (Sweet Chariot), leaving the audience buzzing long after the final chord. Across the nine available tapes, the audience recording gd_nrps70-06-24.aud.pcrp5.23062.sbeok.flacf stands out for its unusually clear crowd ambience and solid capture of this legendary performance.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Five of eight recordings are strongly positive, praising the Dark Star sandwich, tight rhythm section and overall “legendary” feel; three mixed reviews focus mainly on recording fidelity rather than performance issues. Consistent language like ‘transcendent’, ‘on fire’, ‘locked in’ across multiple sources justifies a high positive sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear crowd ambience for an audience tape, good overall fidelity and complete capture of the epic Dark Star sandwich make it the most enjoyable listening experience.

key_highlights: Three‑part Dark Star > Attics Of My Life > Sugar Magnolia sandwich
Not Fade Away into Easy Wind launch
Saint Stephen > China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider seamless medley

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s lead work is described as soaring, smoking and break‑neck; he drives the Dark Star improvisations with transcendent tone throughout.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provides beautiful harmonic depth during Dark Star, locks in solidly with the drums, and “bangs away” on the bass through the jams.
band_performance.Bob: Weir’s rhythmic guitar weaves tightly with Garcia, especially evident in the China Cat > Rider transition; his vocal moments add extra spark.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart is praised as part of a ‘percussion machine’, delivering tight, driving beats that propel every song.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann’s drumming locks solidly with Hart, forming an unmistakable and powerful rhythm foundation.
